bullets.text: Q2 combined ratio of 90.4 and YTD CR of 89.6 with 14% YTD NPW growth; Property running less profitably than target with Q2 CR of 109.0 and YTD CR of 103.6 after $81.5 million aggregate stop-loss recovery.
Total NPW grew 13% for the quarter; YTD NPW growth at 14%; investment portfolio returned 2.2% in Q2 with YTD return of 5.5%, equities returning 17.9% YTD.

Auto frequency decreased 4% in Q2, the 10th consecutive quarterly decline; severity rose 8% for the quarter; YTD bodily injury severity up 8%, leading severity trends; collision severity moderated in Q3.
Significant wind, hail, and tornado activity drove $193 million in YTD Property catastrophe losses through June, up 31% from H1 2018; aggregate stop-loss recoverable totaled $81.5 million YTD.

Progressive Business Owners Policy (BOP) launched through select agents in Ohio; new Property 4.0 product design rolled out in Michigan adding coverage features and improving pricing accuracy; HQX Buy planned for 14 states by year-end.

Vision statement revised to add 'agents' and 'home' — now 'Become consumers' and agents' #1 choice and destination for auto, home, and other insurance' — signaling permanent commitment to agents and home bundling.
Rate revisions applied diligently at state, consumer segment, and product level; Property 4.0 product expected to improve pricing accuracy and serve as foundation for future state rollouts.

Investment portfolio earned 5.5% YTD return through June; fixed income returned 4.4% and equities 17.9% YTD, driven by Fed rate cut expectations and reduction in trade war rhetoric following G20 summit.
